verse.verse_0: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
verse.verse_2: These are the verses of the clear Book.
verse.verse_3: Perhaps, [O Muhammad], you would kill yourself with grief that they will not be believers.
verse.verse_4: If We willed, We could send down to them from the sky a sign for which their necks would remain humbled.
verse.verse_5: And no revelation comes to them anew from the Most Merciful except that they turn away from it.
verse.verse_6: For they have already denied, but there will come to them the news of that which they used to ridicule.
verse.verse_7: Did they not look at the earth - how much We have produced therein from every noble kind?
verse.verse_8: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
verse.verse_9: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
verse.verse_10: And [mention] when your Lord called Moses, [saying], "Go to the wrongdoing people -
verse.verse_11: The people of Pharaoh. Will they not fear Allah?"
verse.verse_12: He said, "My Lord, indeed I fear that they will deny me
verse.verse_13: And that my breast will tighten and my tongue will not be fluent, so send for Aaron.
verse.verse_14: And they have upon me a [claim due to] sin, so I fear that they will kill me."
verse.verse_15: [Allah] said, "No. Go both of you with Our signs; indeed, We are with you, listening.
verse.verse_16: Go to Pharaoh and say, 'We are the messengers of the Lord of the worlds,
verse.verse_17: [Commanded to say], "Send with us the Children of Israel."'"
verse.verse_18: [Pharaoh] said, "Did we not raise you among us as a child, and you remained among us for years of your life?
verse.verse_19: And [then] you did your deed which you did, and you were of the ungrateful."
verse.verse_20: [Moses] said, "I did it, then, while I was of those astray.
verse.verse_21: So I fled from you when I feared you. Then my Lord granted me wisdom and prophethood and appointed me [as one] of the messengers.
verse.verse_22: And is this a favor of which you remind me - that you have enslaved the Children of Israel?"
verse.verse_23: Said Pharaoh, "And what is the Lord of the worlds?"
verse.verse_24: [Moses] said, "The Lord of the heavens and earth and that between them, if you should be convinced."
verse.verse_25: [Pharaoh] said to those around him, "Do you not hear?"
verse.verse_26: [Moses] said, "Your Lord and the Lord of your first forefathers."
verse.verse_27: [Pharaoh] said, "Indeed, your 'messenger' who has been sent to you is mad."
verse.verse_28: [Moses] said, "Lord of the east and the west and that between them, if you were to reason."
verse.verse_29: [Pharaoh] said, "If you take a god other than me, I will surely place you among those imprisoned."
verse.verse_30: [Moses] said, "Even if I brought you proof manifest?"
verse.verse_31: [Pharaoh] said, "Then bring it, if you should be of the truthful."
verse.verse_32: So [Moses] threw his staff, and suddenly it was a serpent manifest.
verse.verse_33: And he drew out his hand; thereupon it was white for the observers.
verse.verse_34: [Pharaoh] said to the eminent ones around him, "Indeed, this is a learned magician.
verse.verse_35: He wants to drive you out of your land by his magic, so what do you advise?"
verse.verse_36: They said, "Postpone [the matter of] him and his brother and send among the cities gatherers
verse.verse_37: Who will bring you every learned, skilled magician."
verse.verse_38: So the magicians were assembled for the appointment of a well-known day.
verse.verse_39: And it was said to the people, "Will you congregate
verse.verse_40: That we might follow the magicians if they are the predominant?"
verse.verse_41: And when the magicians arrived, they said to Pharaoh, "Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?"
verse.verse_42: He said, "Yes, and indeed, you will then be of those near [to me]."
verse.verse_43: Moses said to them, "Throw whatever you will throw."
verse.verse_44: So they threw their ropes and their staffs and said, "By the might of Pharaoh, indeed it is we who are predominant."
verse.verse_45: Then Moses threw his staff, and at once it devoured what they falsified.
verse.verse_46: So the magicians fell down in prostration [to Allah].
verse.verse_47: They said, "We have believed in the Lord of the worlds,
verse.verse_48: The Lord of Moses and Aaron."
verse.verse_49: [Pharaoh] said, "You believed Moses before I gave you permission. Indeed, he is your leader who has taught you magic, but you are going to know. I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will surely crucify you all."
verse.verse_50: They said, "No harm. Indeed, to our Lord we will return.
verse.verse_51: Indeed, we aspire that our Lord will forgive us our sins because we were the first of the believers."
verse.verse_52: And We inspired to Moses, "Travel by night with My servants; indeed, you will be pursued."
verse.verse_53: Then Pharaoh sent among the cities gatherers
verse.verse_54: [And said], "Indeed, those are but a small band,
verse.verse_55: And indeed, they are enraging us,
verse.verse_56: And indeed, we are a cautious society... "
verse.verse_57: So We removed them from gardens and springs
verse.verse_59: Thus. And We caused to inherit it the Children of Israel.
verse.verse_60: So they pursued them at sunrise.
verse.verse_61: And when the two companies saw one another, the companions of Moses said, "Indeed, we are to be overtaken!"
verse.verse_62: [Moses] said, "No! Indeed, with me is my Lord; He will guide me."
verse.verse_63: Then We inspired to Moses, "Strike with your staff the sea," and it parted, and each portion was like a great towering mountain.
verse.verse_64: And We advanced thereto the pursuers.
verse.verse_65: And We saved Moses and those with him, all together.
verse.verse_67: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
verse.verse_68: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
verse.verse_69: And recite to them the news of Abraham,
verse.verse_70: When he said to his father and his people, "What do you worship?"
verse.verse_71: They said, "We worship idols and remain to them devoted."
verse.verse_72: He said, "Do they hear you when you supplicate?
verse.verse_73: Or do they benefit you, or do they harm?"
verse.verse_74: They said, "But we found our fathers doing thus."
verse.verse_75: He said, "Then do you see what you have been worshipping,
verse.verse_77: Indeed, they are enemies to me, except the Lord of the worlds,
verse.verse_78: Who created me, and He [it is who] guides me.
verse.verse_79: And it is He who feeds me and gives me drink.
verse.verse_80: And when I am ill, it is He who cures me
verse.verse_81: And who will cause me to die and then bring me to life
verse.verse_82: And who I aspire that He will forgive me my sin on the Day of Recompense."
verse.verse_83: [And he said], "My Lord, grant me authority and join me with the righteous.
verse.verse_84: And grant me a reputation of honor among later generations.
verse.verse_85: And place me among the inheritors of the Garden of Pleasure.
verse.verse_86: And forgive my father. Indeed, he has been of those astray.
verse.verse_87: And do not disgrace me on the Day they are [all] resurrected -
verse.verse_88: The Day when there will not benefit [anyone] wealth or children
verse.verse_89: But only one who comes to Allah with a sound heart."
verse.verse_90: And Paradise will be brought near [that Day] to the righteous.
verse.verse_91: And Hellfire will be brought forth for the deviators,
verse.verse_92: And it will be said to them, "Where are those you used to worship
verse.verse_93: Other than Allah? Can they help you or help themselves?"
verse.verse_94: So they will be overturned into Hellfire, they and the deviators
verse.verse_95: And the soldiers of Iblees, all together.
verse.verse_96: They will say while they dispute therein,
verse.verse_97: "By Allah, we were indeed in manifest error
verse.verse_98: When we equated you with the Lord of the worlds.
verse.verse_99: And no one misguided us except the criminals.
verse.verse_100: So now we have no intercessors
verse.verse_102: Then if we only had a return [to the world] and could be of the believers... "
verse.verse_103: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
verse.verse_104: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
verse.verse_105: The people of Noah denied the messengers
verse.verse_106: When their brother Noah said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
verse.verse_107: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
verse.verse_108: So fear Allah and obey me.
verse.verse_109: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
verse.verse_110: So fear Allah and obey me."
verse.verse_111: They said, "Should we believe you while you are followed by the lowest [class of people]?"
verse.verse_112: He said, "And what is my knowledge of what they used to do?
verse.verse_113: Their account is only upon my Lord, if you [could] perceive.
verse.verse_114: And I am not one to drive away the believers.
verse.verse_115: I am only a clear warner."
verse.verse_116: They said, "If you do not desist, O Noah, you will surely be of those who are stoned."
verse.verse_117: He said, "My Lord, indeed my people have denied me.
verse.verse_118: Then judge between me and them with decisive judgement and save me and those with me of the believers."
verse.verse_119: So We saved him and those with him in the laden ship.
verse.verse_120: Then We drowned thereafter the remaining ones.
verse.verse_121: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
verse.verse_122: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
verse.verse_124: When their brother Hud said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
verse.verse_125: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
verse.verse_126: So fear Allah and obey me.
verse.verse_127: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
verse.verse_128: Do you construct on every elevation a sign, amusing yourselves,
verse.verse_129: And take for yourselves palaces and fortresses that you might abide eternally?
verse.verse_130: And when you strike, you strike as tyrants.
verse.verse_131: So fear Allah and obey me.
verse.verse_132: And fear He who provided you with that which you know,
verse.verse_133: Provided you with grazing livestock and children
verse.verse_135: Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a terrible day."
verse.verse_136: They said, "It is all the same to us whether you advise or are not of the advisors.
verse.verse_137: This is not but the custom of the former peoples,
verse.verse_138: And we are not to be punished."
verse.verse_139: And they denied him, so We destroyed them. Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
verse.verse_140: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
verse.verse_142: When their brother Salih said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
verse.verse_143: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
verse.verse_144: So fear Allah and obey me.
verse.verse_145: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
verse.verse_146: Will you be left in what is here, secure [from death],
verse.verse_148: And fields of crops and palm trees with softened fruit?
verse.verse_149: And you carve out of the mountains, homes, with skill.
verse.verse_150: So fear Allah and obey me.
verse.verse_151: And do not obey the order of the transgressors,
verse.verse_152: Who cause corruption in the land and do not amend."
verse.verse_153: They said, "You are only of those affected by magic.
verse.verse_154: You are but a man like ourselves, so bring a sign, if you should be of the truthful."
verse.verse_155: He said, "This is a she-camel. For her is a [time of] drink, and for you is a [time of] drink, [each] on a known day.
verse.verse_156: And do not touch her with harm, lest you be seized by the punishment of a terrible day."
verse.verse_157: But they hamstrung her and so became regretful.
verse.verse_158: And the punishment seized them. Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
verse.verse_159: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
verse.verse_160: The people of Lot denied the messengers
verse.verse_161: When their brother Lot said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
verse.verse_162: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
verse.verse_163: So fear Allah and obey me.
verse.verse_164: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
verse.verse_165: Do you approach males among the worlds
verse.verse_166: And leave what your Lord has created for you as mates? But you are a people transgressing."
verse.verse_167: They said, "If you do not desist, O Lot, you will surely be of those evicted."
verse.verse_168: He said, "Indeed, I am, toward your deed, of those who detest [it].
verse.verse_169: My Lord, save me and my family from [the consequence of] what they do."
verse.verse_170: So We saved him and his family, all,
verse.verse_171: Except an old woman among those who remained behind.
verse.verse_173: And We rained upon them a rain [of stones], and evil was the rain of those who were warned.
verse.verse_174: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
verse.verse_175: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
verse.verse_176: The companions of the thicket denied the messengers
verse.verse_177: When Shu'ayb said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
verse.verse_178: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
verse.verse_179: So fear Allah and obey me.
verse.verse_180: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
verse.verse_181: Give full measure and do not be of those who cause loss.
verse.verse_182: And weigh with an even balance.
verse.verse_183: And do not deprive people of their due and do not commit abuse on earth, spreading corruption.
verse.verse_184: And fear He who created you and the former creation."
verse.verse_185: They said, "You are only of those affected by magic.
verse.verse_186: You are but a man like ourselves, and indeed, we think you are among the liars.
verse.verse_187: So cause to fall upon us fragments of the sky, if you should be of the truthful."
verse.verse_188: He said, "My Lord is most knowing of what you do."
verse.verse_189: And they denied him, so the punishment of the day of the black cloud seized them. Indeed, it was the punishment of a terrible day.
verse.verse_190: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
verse.verse_191: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
verse.verse_192: And indeed, the Qur'an is the revelation of the Lord of the worlds.
verse.verse_193: The Trustworthy Spirit has brought it down
verse.verse_194: Upon your heart, [O Muhammad] - that you may be of the warners -
verse.verse_196: And indeed, it is [mentioned] in the scriptures of former peoples.
verse.verse_197: And has it not been a sign to them that it is recognized by the scholars of the Children of Israel?
verse.verse_198: And even if We had revealed it to one among the foreigners
verse.verse_199: And he had recited it to them [perfectly], they would [still] not have been believers in it.
verse.verse_200: Thus have We inserted disbelief into the hearts of the criminals.
verse.verse_201: They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment.
verse.verse_202: And it will come to them suddenly while they perceive [it] not.
verse.verse_203: And they will say, "May we be reprieved?"
verse.verse_204: So for Our punishment are they impatient?
verse.verse_205: Then have you considered if We gave them enjoyment for years
verse.verse_206: And then there came to them that which they were promised?
verse.verse_207: They would not be availed by the enjoyment with which they were provided.
verse.verse_208: And We did not destroy any city except that it had warners
verse.verse_209: As a reminder; and never have We been unjust.
verse.verse_210: And the devils have not brought the revelation down.
verse.verse_211: It is not allowable for them, nor would they be able.
verse.verse_212: Indeed they, from [its] hearing, are removed.
verse.verse_213: So do not invoke with Allah another deity and [thus] be among the punished.
verse.verse_214: And warn, [O Muhammad], your closest kindred.
verse.verse_215: And lower your wing to those who follow you of the believers.
verse.verse_216: And if they disobey you, then say, "Indeed, I am disassociated from what you are doing."
verse.verse_217: And rely upon the Exalted in Might, the Merciful,
verse.verse_218: Who sees you when you arise
verse.verse_219: And your movement among those who prostrate.
verse.verse_220: Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
verse.verse_221: Shall I inform you upon whom the devils descend?
verse.verse_222: They descend upon every sinful liar.
verse.verse_223: They pass on what is heard, and most of them are liars.
verse.verse_224: And the poets - [only] the deviators follow them;
verse.verse_225: Do you not see that in every valley they roam
verse.verse_226: And that they say what they do not do? -
verse.verse_227: Except those [poets] who believe and do righteous deeds and remember Allah often and defend [the Muslims] after they were wronged. And those who have wronged are going to know to what [kind of] return they will be returned.
